title: AI Engineer - Full-Stack & Applied UI
company_focus:
AI-powered data unification and master data management (MDM)
SaaS platform
description:
At Reltio®, we believe data should fuel business success.
Reltio’s AI-powered data unification and management
capabilities—encompassing entity resolution, multi-domain
master data management (MDM), and data products—transform
siloed data from disparate sources into unified, trusted,
and interoperable data. Reltio Data Cloud™ delivers
interoperable data where and when it's needed, empowering
data and analytics leaders with unparalleled business
responsiveness. Leading enterprise brands—across multiple
industries around the globe—rely on our award-winning data
unification and cloud-native MDM capabilities to improve
efficiency, manage risk and drive growth.

At Reltio, our values guide everything we do. With an
unyielding commitment to prioritizing our “Customer First”,
we strive to ensure their success. We embrace our
differences and are “Better Together” as One Reltio. We are
always looking to “Simplify and Share” our knowledge when we
collaborate to remove obstacles for each other. We hold
ourselves accountable for our actions and outcomes and
strive for excellence. We “Own It”. Every day, we innovate
and evolve, so that today is “Always Better Than Yesterday”.
If you share and embody these values, we invite you to join
our team at Reltio and contribute to our mission of
excellence.

Reltio has earned numerous awards and top rankings for our
technology, our culture and our people. Reltio was founded
on a distributed workforce and offers flexible work
arrangements to help our people manage their personal and
professional lives. If you’re ready to work on unrivaled
technology where your desire to be part of a collaborative
team is met with a laser-focused mission to enable digital
transformation with connected data, let’s talk!

Job Summary:

We are building a highly specialized Enterprise AI Hub to
engineer the "Reltio Brain"—a Context Intelligence Operating
System that transforms fragmented institutional knowledge
into governed, autonomous action.

As the AI Engineer (Full-Stack & Applied UI), you are the
critical bridge between complex AI reasoning systems and the
human workforce. While backend systems handle the heavy
lifting of vector search and agentic orchestration, you will
be a senior engineer delivering end-to-end solutions and
patterns within the established Enterprise AI framework. You
will build the internal sandboxes, generative user
interfaces, and workflow applications that allow Reltio’s
embedded AI Business Partners to interact seamlessly with
our AI ecosystem.

This role requires a "hacker-minded" builder who leverages
modern AI coding assistants (like Cursor, Codex, and Claude
Code) to dramatically accelerate front-end development,
translating functional business requirements into polished,
secure internal tools in days, not weeks.

Job Duties and Responsibilities:

Generative Interface Development:

- Lead complex implementations of highly responsive, intuitive
  front-end applications and custom agent dashboards that
  consume streaming LLM responses and complex agentic
  outputs.

Rapid Prototyping & AI-Assisted Coding:

- Leverage modern AI coding assistants (e.g., Cursor, Claude
  Code, Codex) to rapidly iterate on prototypes.
- You will act as the technical execution engine for embedded
  AI Business Partners, turning their defined workflows into
  functional realities.
- Support defining and establishing the AI SDLC for
  implementing best practice engineering patterns via agents
  to align to and accelerate established development,
  quality, and release processes.

Full-Stack Connectivity:

- Operate with autonomy within defined domain boundaries to
  connect modern front-end frameworks to backend Model
  Context Protocol (MCP) gateways and vector search APIs.
- Ensure seamless authentication and state management across
  personal, team, and enterprise context layers.

Component Library Creation:

- Contribute to reusable UI components, RAG retrieval modules,
  and generative UI templates—executing within established
  architectural standards to maintain the look, feel, and
  interaction patterns of all internal AI tools.

Human-in-the-Loop Integration:

- Build secure approval workflows and UX patterns that allow
  human operators to review, modify, or veto high-stakes
  actions proposed by autonomous agents before execution.

Skills You Must Have:

- 5+ years of full-stack software development with a heavy
  emphasis on modern frontend ecosystems.
- Expert-level proficiency in TypeScript, JavaScript, React,
  and Next.js.
- Extensive experience deploying and scaling applications on
  platforms like Vercel, particularly utilizing the Vercel
  AI SDK for streaming text and generative UI components.
- Strong capability in utilizing AI coding tools (e.g., Claude
  Code, Codex, Cursor) to dramatically accelerate personal
  output while maintaining strict code quality and security
  standards.
- A deep empathy for user experience and an understanding of
  how to design interfaces that build trust between human
  workers and autonomous systems.

Skills That Are Nice to Have:

- Familiarity with backend Python development to collaborate
  effectively with backend/orchestration engineers.
- Experience in a 500–2,000 employee SaaS company or similar
  scale.
- Familiarity with OAuth and implementing Role-Based Access
  Control (RBAC) and Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC)
  within UI layers to govern data access across L1/L2/L3
  contexts.
- Working knowledge of deploying applications that interact
  securely with enterprise systems like Salesforce, Jira, or
  NetSuite.

Reltio is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace. We are
committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of
race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin,
sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status,
disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also
consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal
histories, consistent with legal requirements. Reltio is
committed to working with and providing reasonable
accommodation to applicants with physical and mental
disabilities.
responsibilities: Build responsive front-end applications and custom agent dashboards that consume streaming LLM responses and agentic outputs
Rapidly prototype and iterate using AI coding assistants (Cursor, Claude Code, Codex) to translate business workflows into functional tools
Connect modern front-end frameworks to backend MCP gateways and vector search APIs with seamless authentication and state management
Create reusable UI components, RAG retrieval modules, and generative UI templates within established architectural standards
Build secure human-in-the-loop approval workflows allowing operators to review, modify, or veto autonomous agent actions
Support defining and establishing the AI SDLC for engineering patterns via agents to align with development and release processes
use_cases: Enterprise AI Hub (Reltio Brain) — a Context Intelligence Operating System that transforms institutional knowledge into governed autonomous action
Agent dashboards for embedded AI Business Partners to interact with the AI ecosystem
Generative user interfaces for streaming LLM and agentic output consumption
RAG-based retrieval modules for enterprise knowledge access
Human-in-the-loop approval workflows for high-stakes autonomous agent actions
ai_classification_reasoning:
This role involves building generative UI applications that
consume streaming LLM responses, connecting frontends to MCP
gateways and vector search APIs, building RAG retrieval
modules, and creating human-in-the-loop workflows for
autonomous agents. While it's a full-stack/UI-heavy role, it
directly implements and delivers AI-powered systems (agent
dashboards, LLM streaming interfaces, RAG components) —
making it ai-first rather than ai-support.
